text: Rainwater harvesting captures roof runoff for household use. A 1,000 square foot roof in an area with 30 inches of annual rainfall can collect approximately 18,000 gallons per year (1 inch of rain on 1 sq ft = 0.623 gallons). Use food-grade storage tanks, install a first-flush diverter to discard the initial dirty runoff, and cover all openings with fine mesh to prevent mosquito breeding. Filter and disinfect before drinking.

Well water should be tested annually for coliform bacteria, nitrates, pH, and total dissolved solids. Contact your county health department for testing services. Common well problems: bacterial contamination (treat with shock chlorination — pour 3 pints of unscented household bleach per 100 gallons of well water, let sit 12-24 hours, then flush), iron staining (install an iron filter), and hard water (install a water softener).

Gravity-fed water systems use elevation difference to create water pressure without electricity. You need approximately 2.31 feet of elevation for every 1 PSI of water pressure. A typical household needs 20-40 PSI, so the water source or storage tank should be 46-92 feet above the point of use. Use polyethylene pipe rated for your pressure needs. For every 100 feet of horizontal pipe run, add 5% to account for friction loss.

For emergency water purification, boiling is the most reliable method. Bring water to a rolling boil for 1 minute (3 minutes above 6,500 feet elevation). Chemical treatment: add 6 drops (1/8 teaspoon) of unscented liquid household bleach (5-6% sodium hypochlorite) per gallon of clear water, or 12 drops per gallon of cloudy water. Stir and let stand 30 minutes — the water should have a slight chlorine smell. If not, repeat the dose and wait another 15 minutes.

A spring development captures groundwater where it naturally emerges. Excavate uphill from the spring emergence point to find the source. Install a spring box — a concrete or stone collection chamber — around the source. The box should have a watertight cover, an overflow pipe, a drain pipe, and a screened outlet pipe leading to your storage tank. Divert surface water away from the spring box with a drainage ditch uphill. Have the water tested before drinking.
